items.title: June 26, 1996: With the fourth pick in the NBA Draft, the Milwaukee Bucks select Stephon Marbury from Georgia Tech.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 26, 1996: With the fourth pick in the NBA Draft, the Milwaukee Bucks select Stephon Marbury from Georgia Tech.

Less than an hour later, it was announced that Marbury had been traded to the Minnesota Timberwolves for Ray Allen and a first round pick in the 1998 NBA Draft.

items.title: Asked Lakers 1st round pick Cameron Carr about his father Chris Carr, who played for six seasons with six teams
items.author: Law Murray 📘
items.body_excerpt:
Asked Lakers 1st round pick Cameron Carr about his father Chris Carr, who played for six seasons with six teams

"Once I finally fell in love with it and found my *why* on why I wanted to play basketball, then I feel like I could go to him with more conversation, connection, or questions."

items.title: Sources: The Utah Jazz plan to sign Tamar Bates to a two-way deal. He was sidelined with a foot injury as a two-way rookie for the Nuggets after averaging 19.5 points on 55% overall and 44% from 3-point range, 4.4 boards, 2.3 assists, and 1.5 steals in the G League last season.
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: Sources: The Utah Jazz plan to sign Tamar Bates to a two-way deal. He was sidelined with a foot injury as a two-way rookie for the Nuggets after averaging 19.5 points on 55% overall and 44% from 3-point range, 4.4 boards, 2.3 assists, and 1.5 steals in the G League last season.
